title: Chapter 11 - Divs & Spans
description: Check understanding of divs and spans.

questions.prompt: Which element is block-level by default?

explanations.overview: <div> is a block-level container; <span> is inline.

byOptionId.div: Correct — <div> is block-level and takes full width by default.
byOptionId.span: Incorrect — <span> is inline and flows within lines of text.
byOptionId.img: Incorrect — is inline (or replaced) by default, not a block container.
byOptionId.a: Incorrect — <a> is inline; it creates links not block-level layout.

options.label: To wrap small inline parts of text for styling or scripts

byOptionId.inline: Correct — <span> is for inline grouping and small styling hooks.
byOptionId.large: Incorrect — use block containers like <div> or semantic sections for large sections.
byOptionId.image: Incorrect — embeds images; does not replace it.
